probe: ADVERSARIAL verification of wpb_integrability.json (DNW13 Eq-11 Buchert invariant alpha^2 constancy on the tracker vs forced/observed histories)

2_tracker_constancy.interpretation: alpha^2 constant to machine/precision floor and SHRINKS as precision rises => the tracker is an exact Buchert solution and the Eq-11 transcription is faithful. The wall-reading drift shows the bare-abar reading is genuinely pinned by the tracker.

3_4_numeric_histories.method: independent pipeline: dz/dtau = np.gradient(solver z(tau)); f_v'' = one np.gradient of f_v'(tau); unnormalised abar (spread convention-free)

5_reproduction.note: committed W.solve_and_alpha2 / W.drift / W.tracker_alpha2_analytic re-run in-memory (main() NOT called; committed JSON not touched).

7_eightyone_pct_adjudication.notes_sec8_source: deleted prototype proto_modelv4.py illustrative SMOOTH forced history
7_eightyone_pct_adjudication.assessment: The specific 81% figure is NOT reproduced for the actual Probe-R V.fv_nodes; both the JSON and this verification confirm the fitted nodes drift MORE (interior ~1.5x, span ~15.5x). The JSON discloses this at full volume in verdict.note_on_81pct. The 81% came from a different (deleted, smoother) input history, so its non-reproduction is an input-provenance mismatch, not a transcription error. The load-bearing claim -- forced drift >> tracker, grossly non-constant -- is confirmed and representation-independent.

verdict.summary: Transcription faithful (3-way string match); tracker alpha^2 constant to the precision floor and SHRINKS with precision (60-digit 0.0e+00 << float64 1.8e-15) => transcription VALIDATED; bare-abar reading pinned (wall reading drifts 1.37); forced & observed drift O(100-1000%) >> tracker. The one documented gap is the 81% figure, which the JSON itself discloses as a non-reproduction of a deleted-prototype input, not a transcription defect.
